If char is a number, this function interprets it as the ASCII value of a character. If char is a floating-point number, this function rounds to the nearest integer. Otherwise, this function returns FALSE.

inputs.name: char
inputs.description: char can be a scalar string or number, clusters of strings or numbers, arrays of strings or numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: hex?
outputs.description: hex? is a Boolean value of the same data type structure as char.

name: Coerce To Type Function
description: Converts the input data to a compatible data type while preserving the data value. Unlike the Type Cast function, this function does not reinterpret the input data.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description:
type specifies the data type to which you want to convert the input data x.
    To set the data type to which you want to convert the input data, wire a constant or control of the desired data type to type. LabVIEW ignores any data in the constant or control wired to type.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description:
x specifies the data you want to convert.
    This input accepts any data type.

outputs.name: coerced x
outputs.description: coerced x returns the value of the input data x after the conversion.

name: Unbundle
description: Splits a cluster into each of its individual elements.

inputs.name: cluster
inputs.description: cluster is the cluster whose elements you want to access.

name: Fixed-Point to Integer Cast Function
description: Scales the fixed-point input to produce an integer output. This function is the equivalent of an arithmetic shift of the input, constrained so that the least significant bit of the input becomes the least significant bit of the output.

inputs.name: fixed-point
inputs.description: fixed-point is the data you want to scale. fixed-point can be any fixed-point data type.

outputs.name: integer
outputs.description: integer is the input data scaled to the smallest compatible integer type that prevents overflow. This output discards the overflow status.

name: Stop Function
description: Stops the VI in which it executes, just as if you clicked the Abort Execution button on the toolbar. Before you call this function with a TRUE input, be sure to complete all final tasks for the VI first, such as closing files, setting safe values for devices being controlled, and so on.

inputs.name: stop? (T)
inputs.description: stop? is the Boolean value that determines if the VI stops.If you wire an error cluster to stop? and an error occurs, the error cluster passes a TRUE value to the function.

name: Get Date/Time In Seconds Function
description: Returns a timestamp of the current time. LabVIEW calculates this timestamp using the number of seconds elapsed since 12:00 a.m., Friday, January 1, 1904, Universal Time.

outputs.name: current time
outputs.description: current time returns a timestamp of the LabVIEW system time. LabVIEW calculates this timestamp using the number of seconds elapsed since 12:00 a.m., Friday, January 1, 1904, Universal Time [01-01-1904 00:00:00].

name: Quit LabVIEW Function
description: Stops all executing VIs and ends the current instance of LabVIEW.

inputs.name: quit? (T)
inputs.description: If quit? is TRUE (default), LabVIEW quits.If you wire an error cluster to quit? and an error occurs, the error cluster passes a TRUE value to the function.

name: Interleave 1D Arrays
description: Interleaves corresponding elements from the input arrays into a single output array.

inputs.name: array
inputs.description: array 0..n-1 must be 1D. If the input array is not the same size, the number of elements in interleaved array equals the number of elements in the smallest input array multiplied by the number of input arrays.

outputs.name: interleaved array
outputs.description:
interleaved array[0] contains array 0[0], interleaved array[1] contains array 1[0], interleaved array[n-1] contains array n-1[0], interleaved array[n] contains array 0[1], and so on, where n is the number of input terminals.
    The following table shows how elements from the input arrays affect int

name: And Function
description: Computes the logical AND of the inputs. Both inputs must be Boolean values, numeric values, or error clusters. If both inputs are TRUE, the function returns TRUE. Otherwise, it returns FALSE.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x must be a Boolean value or a number.x can be a scalar, array or cluster of numbers or Boolean values, array of clusters of numbers or Boolean values, and so on. If x is an error cluster, only the status parameter of the error cluster passes to the input terminal.

inputs.name: y
inputs.description: y must be a Boolean value or a number. y can be a scalar, array or cluster of numbers or Boolean values, arrays of clusters of numbers or Boolean values, and so on. If y is an error cluster, only the status parameter of the error cluster passes to the input terminal.

outputs.name: x .and. y?
outputs.description: x .and. y? is the logical AND of x and y.

name: Rotate Left With Carry Function
description: Rotates each bit in the input value one bit to the left (from least significant to most significant bit), inserts carry in the low-order bit, and returns the most significant bit.

inputs.name: carry
inputs.description: carry is the new low-order bit of value.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: value must be an integer. It cannot be an array or a cluster.

outputs.name: msb carry out
outputs.description: msb carry out is the former high-order bit of value.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: value is the new value.The data type of the output value is determined by the data type of the input value.

name: Index & Bundle Cluster Array
description: Indexes a set of arrays and creates a cluster array in which the ith element contains the ith element of each input array.

inputs.name: array
inputs.description: array of x..z can be a 1D array of any type. Array inputs do not all have to be the same type.

outputs.name: array of clusters
outputs.description:
array of cluster is a cluster array containing one element from each input array. The number of elements in the output array equals the number of elements in the shortest input array.
  


    
    
  —

name: Build Cluster Array
description: Bundles each element input into a cluster and assembles all element clusters into an array of clusters.

inputs.name: element
inputs.description: element 0..n-1 inputs must be of the same type as the value wired to the topmost element terminal.

outputs.name: array of clusters
outputs.description:
array of clusters is the resulting array. Each cluster contains a single element.
    


    
    
  —

name: Logarithm Base X Function
description: Computes the base x logarithm of y.

inputs.name: y
inputs.description: y can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: logx(y)
outputs.description:
logx(y) is the logarithm of y to the base x. When x and y are complex, the following equation defines logx(y):
    logx(y) = ln(y)/ln(x)
    Refer to the Natural Logarithm function for more information.

name: Inverse Hyperbolic Sine Function
description: Computes the inverse hyperbolic sine of x.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: argsinh(x)
outputs.description: argsinh(x) is of the same numeric representation as x.

name: Logarithm Base 2 Function
description: Computes the base 2 logarithm of x.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: log2(x)
outputs.description: log2(x) is of the same numeric representation as x. When x is of the form x = a + bi, that is, when x is complex, the following equation defines log2(x):

name: Scan From String
description: Scans the input string and converts the string according to format string.

inputs.name: format string
inputs.description: format string specifies how to convert the input string into the output arguments.The default is to scan the string according to the default format for the data types of the outputs. Formatting a time stamp as anything other than time returns an error. Right-click the function and select Edit Scan S

inputs.name: input string
inputs.description: input string is the string, path, enumerated type, time stamp, or numeric data to scan.

inputs.name: initial scan location
inputs.description: initial scan location is the offset into the string where the scan begins.The default is 0.

inputs.name: error in
inputs.description: error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality.

outputs.name: remaining string
outputs.description: remaining string returns the portion of the string that remains after scanning all arguments.

outputs.name: offset past scan
outputs.description: offset past scan is the offset of input string after completing the scan.

outputs.name: error out
outputs.description: error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality.

name: Cast Unit Bases Function
description: Changes the base units associated with the input to the base units associated with unit and returns the results at the output terminal.

inputs.name: unit (none)
inputs.description:
unit associates input with output results.
    
    The default is none, which does not associate any units.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x is a floating-point number with or without a unit.

outputs.name: x
outputs.description: x returns the same data type and structure as x.

name: To More Generic Class Function
description: Typecasts a reference, such as a control or a type definition, to a more generic class or interface in the inheritance hierarchy.

inputs.name: target class
inputs.description: target class is the class or interface to which you want to upcast reference.You can wire a class specifier constant or any wire of the target type to this input.

inputs.name: reference
inputs.description: reference is the refnum or LabVIEW class or interface to upcast.

outputs.name: generic class reference
outputs.description:
generic class reference is the upcasted reference.
    If an error occurs, generic class reference is Not A Refnum.

name: Inverse Hyperbolic Cosecant Function
description: Computes the inverse hyperbolic cosecant of x.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: argcsch(x)
outputs.description: argcsch(x) is of the same numeric representation as x.

name: Array Size Function
description: Returns the number of elements in each dimension of array.

inputs.name: array
inputs.description: array can be an n-dimensional array of any type.

outputs.name: size(s)
outputs.description:
size(s) is a 32-bit integer if array is one-dimensional (1D).
      If array is multidimensional, the returned value is a 1D array in which each element is a 32-bit integer that represents the number of elements in the corresponding dimension of array. The order of elements in the return array corre

name: In Range and Coerce Function
description: Determines whether x falls within a range specified by the upper limit and lower limit inputs and optionally coerces the value to fall within the range. The function performs the coercion only in Compare Elements mode. This function accepts time stamp values if all inputs are time stamp values. You can change the comparison mode of this function.

inputs.name: upper limit
inputs.description: upper limit of the range.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x is the value to check and/or coerce.

inputs.name: lower limit
inputs.description: lower limit of the range.

outputs.name: coerced(x)
outputs.description: coerced(x) returns the coerced or unchanged value of x. If x is within the range set by the upper limit and lower limit inputs or if the function is in Compare Aggregates mode, the value is unchanged. If x is not in range and the function is in Compare Elements mode, the function converts the value

outputs.name: In Range?
outputs.description: In Range? is a Boolean value in Compare Aggregates mode. In Compare Elements mode, the data type structure of In Range? matches the data type structure of x, with each scalar replaced by a Boolean value. If upper limit, x, or lower limit is NaN, In Range? is FALSE.

name: To Fixed-Point Function
description: Converts any non-complex number to fixed-point representation.

inputs.name: fixed-point type
inputs.description: fixed-point type is the fixed-point data type to which you want to convert the integer data. You can configure the fixed-point data type in the Data Type page of the Properties dialog box. LabVIEW ignores any data in the constant or control that you wire to fixed-point type. You also can leave this

inputs.name: number
inputs.description: number can be any non-complex number.

outputs.name: fixed-point
outputs.description: fixed-point is the input data scaled to the requested fixed-point data type.

name: To Long Integer Function
description: Converts a number to a 32-bit integer in the range -(2^ 31) to (2^ 31)-1. This function rounds all floating-point and fixed-point numeric values to the nearest integer.

inputs.name: number
inputs.description: number can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: 32bit integer
outputs.description: 32bit integer is of the same data type structure as number.

name: Natural Logarithm (Arg +1) Function
description: Computes the natural logarithm of (x + 1).

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: ln(x+1)
outputs.description: ln(x+1) is of the same numeric representation as x. When x is of the form x = a + bi, that is, when x is complex, the following equation defines ln(x+1): ln(x+1) = ln(|x + 1|) + i arctan2(b, a + 1)

name: Exponential Function
description: Computes the value of e raised to the x power, or the exponential of x.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: exp(x)
outputs.description:
exp(x) is of the same numeric representation as x. The following equation defines the exponential exp(x):
    exp(a+bi) = exp(a)(cos(b)+i sin(b))
    when x is of the form x = a + bi, that is, when x is complex.

name: Date/Time To Seconds Function
description: Creates a timestamp from a set of individual values that specify a date and time.

inputs.name: date time rec
inputs.description:
date time rec is the date and time to convert. If is UTC is TRUE, date time rec is in Universal Time. If is UTC is FALSE, date time rec is in the configured time zone for the computer.
  


    
    fractional second
  —
    
    fractional second must be greater than or equal to 0 and less than 1.

inputs.name: fractional second
inputs.description: fractional second must be greater than or equal to 0 and less than 1.

inputs.name: second
inputs.description: second can be from 0 to 59.

inputs.name: minute
inputs.description: minute can be from 0 to 59.

inputs.name: hour
inputs.description: hour can be from 0 to 23.

inputs.name: day of month
inputs.description: day of month can be from 1 to 31.

inputs.name: month
inputs.description: month can be from 1 to 12.

inputs.name: year
inputs.description: year can be from 1600 to 3000.

inputs.name: day of week
inputs.description:
day of week can be from 1 to 7, which correspond to Sunday and Saturday, respectively.
    Note This function ignores day of week when building the timestamp.

inputs.name: day of year
inputs.description:
day of year can be from 1 to 366.
    Note This function ignores day of year when building the timestamp.

inputs.name: DST
inputs.description: DST indicates whether the time is standard (0) or daylight savings time (1). You also can set DST to -1 to have the VI determine the correct time automatically each time you run the VI. If is UTC is TRUE, the function ignores the DST setting and uses Universal Time.

inputs.name: is UTC (F)
inputs.description: is UTC specifies if date time rec is in Universal Time or in the configured time zone for the computer. If TRUE, date time rec is in Universal Time. The default is FALSE.

outputs.name: time stamp
outputs.description:
time stamp is the time that represents the date and time specified by the individual values in date time rec.
    If year and month are out of range, the results are unpredictable.

name: Number To Boolean Array Function
description: Converts an integer or fixed-point number to a Boolean array. If you wire an integer to number, Boolean array returns an array of 8, 16, 32, or 64 elements, depending on the number of bits in the integer. If you wire a fixed-point number to number, the size of the array that Boolean array returns equals the word length of the fixed-point number. The 0th element of the array corresponds to the least significant bit of the two's complement representation of the integer.

inputs.name: number
inputs.description: number can be a number.

outputs.name: Boolean array
outputs.description: Boolean array can have 8, 16, 32, or 64 elements if number is an integer. If number is a fixed-point number, Boolean array can have between 1 and 64 elements.

name: Expression Node
description: Use the Expression Node to calculate expressions that contain a single variable. The following built-in functions are allowed in formulas: abs, acos, acosh, asin, asinh, atan, atanh, ceil, cos, cosh, cot, csc, exp, expm1, floor, getexp, getman, int, intrz, ln, lnp1, log, log2, max, min, mod, rand, rem, sec, sign, sin, sinc, sinh, sizeOfDim, sqrt, tan, tanh.

inputs.description: input is the value that the Expression Node uses as the variable.

outputs.description: output returns the value of the calculation.

name: And Array Elements Function
description: Returns TRUE if all the elements in Boolean array are TRUE or if Boolean array is empty. Otherwise, the function returns FALSE. This function accepts an array of any size but returns only a single value based on all values in Boolean array.

inputs.name: Boolean array
inputs.description: Boolean array  can have any number of dimensions.

outputs.name: logical AND
outputs.description: logical AND indicates if all elements in Boolean array are TRUE.

name: Less Or Equal? Function
description: Returns TRUE if x is less than or equal to y. Otherwise, this function returns FALSE. You can change the comparison mode of this function.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x is a value to compare to y

inputs.name: y
inputs.description: y must be of the same type as x

outputs.name: x <= y?
outputs.description: x ≤ y? returns the Boolean result of the operation. When you compare arrays, x ≤ y? is a scalar in Compare Aggregates mode and a Boolean array in Compare Elements mode (default).

name: Sort 1D Array Function
description: Returns a sorted version of array with the elements arranged in ascending order. If array is an array of clusters, the function sorts the elements by comparing the first elements. If the first elements match, the function compares the second and subsequent elements. The connector pane displays the default data types for this polymorphic function.

inputs.name: array
inputs.description: unsorted array can be a 1D array of any type.

outputs.name: sorted array
outputs.description: sorted array is the output array.

name: Get Matrix Elements
description: Returns the elements of matrix at row and column.

inputs.name: matrix
inputs.description: matrix must be a 2D array of any numeric type or a real or complex matrix.

inputs.name: index (row)
inputs.description:
row must be an integer, a floating-point numeric, a 1D array of integers, or a 1D array of a floating-point numeric.
    The default is a 1D array of integers that contains all row indexes.

inputs.name: index (col)
inputs.description:
column must be an integer, a floating-point numeric, a 1D array of integers, or a 1D array of a floating-point numeric.
    The default is 0.

outputs.name: output matrix
outputs.description: element is the scalar, matrix, or 2D array that contains the matrix elements. The data type of element is the same as the data type of matrix.

description: Interpolates points in a 1D array that represents a 2D non-descending graph. inputs.name: array of numbers or points
inputs.description: array of numbers or points can be an array of numbers or an array of points where each point is a cluster of x- and y-coordinates.If this input is an array of points, this function uses the second elements in the clusters, or the y-coordinates, to obtain a fractional index that it then uses to inter

inputs.name: threshold y
inputs.description: threshold y is the threshold value for the function. If threshold y is less than or equal to the array value at start index, the function returns start index for fractional index or x.If threshold y is greater than every value in the array, the function returns the index of the last value. If the ar

inputs.name: start index (0)
inputs.description: start index must be a number. The default is 0, which means the function returns the result calculated from the entire array, rather than a specified section of the array.

outputs.name: fractional index or x
outputs.description: fractional index or x is the interpolated result LabVIEW calculates for the array of numbers or points 1D input array.For example, suppose array of numbers or points is an array of four numbers [4, 5, 5, 6], start index is 0, and threshold y is 5. The fractional index or x is 1, corresponding to the

name: To Double Precision Float Function
description: Converts a number to a double-precision, floating-point number.

inputs.name: number
inputs.description: number can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, a time stamp, and so on.

outputs.name: double precision float
outputs.description: double precision float is of the same data type structure as number.

name: To Unsigned Word Integer Function
description: Converts a number to a 16-bit unsigned integer in the range 0 to 65,535.

inputs.name: number
inputs.description: number can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: unsigned 16bit integer
outputs.description: unsigned 16bit integer is of the same data type structure as number.

name: Compound Arithmetic
description: Performs arithmetic on one or more numeric, array, cluster, or Boolean inputs. To select the operation (Add, Multiply, AND, OR, or XOR), right-click the function and select Change Mode from the shortcut menu. When you select this function from the Numeric palette, the default mode is Add. When you select this function from the Boolean palette, the default mode is OR.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description:
value 0..n-1 can be a number or Boolean value, an array of numbers or Boolean values, a cluster, array of clusters, and so on.
    You can wire a waveform to only one value input. If an input is a waveform, you can have an unlimited number of scalar inputs of varying sizes. If value is an error clus

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: result returns the result of the selected operation applied to the value 0..n-1. For AND, OR, or XOR, result returns the bitwise operations on numeric inputs and logical operations on Boolean inputs.

name: To Unsigned Byte Integer Function
description: Converts a number to an 8-bit unsigned integer in the range 0 to 255.

inputs.name: number
inputs.description: number can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: unsigned 8bit integer
outputs.description: unsigned 8bit integer is of the same data type structure as number.

name: Less? Function
description: Returns TRUE if x is less than y. Otherwise, this function returns FALSE. You can change the comparison mode of this function.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x is a value to compare to y

inputs.name: y
inputs.description: y must be of the same type as x

outputs.name: x < y?
outputs.description: x < y? returns the Boolean result of the operation. When you compare arrays, x < y? is a scalar in Compare Aggregates mode and a Boolean array in Compare Elements mode (default).

name: To Upper Case Function
description: Converts all alphabetic characters in string to uppercase characters. Evaluates all numbers in string as ASCII codes for characters. This function does not affect non-alphabetic characters.

inputs.name: string
inputs.description: string can be a string, a cluster of strings, an array of strings, or an array of clusters of strings.

outputs.name: all upper case string
outputs.description: all upper case string has the same structure as string.

name: VISA Read To File Function
description: Reads byte count synchronously and stores the transferred data in the file specified by filename.

inputs.name: VISA resource name
inputs.description: VISA resource name specifies the resource to be opened. The VISA resource name control also specifies the session and class.

inputs.name: filename
inputs.description: filename is the name of file to which data will be written.

inputs.name: byte count
inputs.description: byte count is the number of bytes to be read.

inputs.name: error in (no error)
inputs.description: error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. This input provides standard error in functionality.

outputs.name: VISA resource name out
outputs.description: VISA resource name out is a copy of the VISA resource name that VISA functions return.

outputs.name: return count
outputs.description: return count is the number of bytes actually transferred.

outputs.name: error out
outputs.description: error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality.

name: Array Max & Min Function
description: Returns the maximum and minimum values found in array, along with the indexes for each value.

inputs.name: array
inputs.description: array can be an n-dimensional array of any type.

outputs.name: max value
outputs.description: max value is of the same data type and structure as the elements in array.

outputs.name: max index(es)
outputs.description:
max index(es) is the index for the first max value.
    If array is multidimensional, max index(es) is an array whose elements are the indexes for the first maximum value in array.

outputs.name: min value
outputs.description: min value is of the same data type and structure as the elements in array.

outputs.name: min index(es)
outputs.description:
min index(es) is the index for the first min value.
    If array is multidimensional, min index(es) is an array whose elements are the indexes for the first minimum value in array.

name: Scale By Power Of 2 Function
description: Multiplies x by 2 raised to the power of n.

inputs.description: n can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.If n is a floating-point number, this function rounds n before it scales x (0.5 rounds to 0; 0.51 rounds to 1). If n is a 64-bit integer, LabVIEW coerces n to a 32-bit integer.

inputs.name: x

outputs.name: x*2^n

name: Split 1D Array Function
description: Divides array at index and returns the two portions with the element of index at the beginning of second subarray.

inputs.name: array
inputs.description: array can be a 1D array of any type.

inputs.name: index
inputs.description: index must be numeric. If index is negative or 0, first subarray is empty.If index is equal to or greater than the size of array, second subarray is empty.

outputs.name: first subarray
outputs.description: first subarray contains array[0] through array[index-1].

outputs.name: second subarray
outputs.description: second subarray contains the remaining array elements not already contained in first subarray.

name: Absolute Value Function
description: Returns the absolute value of the input.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description:
x can be a scalar number, a fixed-point number, an array or cluster of numbers, an array of clusters of numbers, and so on.
    x cannot be an unsigned integer, because unsigned integers represent only non-negative integers.

outputs.name: abs(x)
outputs.description:
abs(x) is the absolute value of x.

name: Spreadsheet String To Array Function
description: Converts the spreadsheet string to an array of the dimension and representation of array type. This function works for arrays of strings and arrays of numbers.

inputs.name: delimiter (Tab)
inputs.description: delimiter specifies the value or values used to separate fields in the spreadsheet file. The default is a single tab character. You can specify multiple delimiters by wiring an array of strings to delimiter. Each element of the array should contain a different delimiter.For example, if you wire an a

inputs.name: format string
inputs.description: format string specifies how to convert the spreadsheet string into an array. Use %s to convert spreadsheet string to an array of strings. Use %d or %f to convert spreadsheet string to an array of numbers.Use the format string syntax.

inputs.name: spreadsheet string
inputs.description: spreadsheet string contains numeric or string values separated into columns by delimiters, such as tabs or commas, with an end-of-line (EOL) character separating rows.The function converts each element in spreadsheet string according to format string and stores the resulting values in array.

inputs.name: array type (2D Dbl)
inputs.description: array type is the data type input for the array. The default is a 2D array of double-precision, floating-point numbers.Refer to Array To Spreadsheet String for information about the spreadsheet format expected for arrays of three dimensions or more.

outputs.name: array
outputs.description: array is spreadsheet string converted to an array of the dimension and representation of array type.

name: Greater Or Equal To 0? Function
description: Returns TRUE if x is greater than or equal to 0. Otherwise, this function returns FALSE.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x can be a numeric scalar value, cluster, array of numbers, or a time stamp value.

outputs.name: x >= 0?
outputs.description: x >= 0? is a Boolean value of the same data type structure as x.

name: Exponential Function
description: Computes the value of e raised to the x power, or the exponential of x.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: exp(x)
outputs.description:
exp(x) is of the same numeric representation as x. The following equation defines the exponential exp(x):
    exp(a+bi) = exp(a)(cos(b)+i sin(b))
    when x is of the form x = a + bi, that is, when x is complex.

name: Call Parent Class Method

Because LabVIEW uses ActiveX technology to implement MATLAB script nodes, they are available only on Windows.

name: Greater? Function
description: Returns TRUE if x is greater than y. Otherwise, this function returns FALSE. You can change the comparison mode of this function.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x is a value to compare to y

inputs.name: y
inputs.description: y must be of the same type as x

outputs.name: x > y?
outputs.description: x > y? returns the Boolean result of the operation.When you compare arrays, x > y? is a scalar in Compare Aggregates mode and a Boolean array in Compare Elements mode (default).

name: Format Date/Time String Function
description: Displays a timestamp value or a numeric value as time in the format you specify using time format codes.

inputs.name: time format string (%c)
inputs.description: time format string specifies the format of the output string.Time format codes (beginning with %) not recognized by the function as a format code return the character literally. The default code is %c, which corresponds to the date/time representation appropriate for the configured time zone for the

inputs.name: time stamp
inputs.description: time stamp can be a timestamp or a numeric. If numeric, this number is the time-zone-independent number of seconds that have elapsed since 12:00 a.m., Friday, January 1, 1904, Universal Time [01-01-1904 00:00:00]. The default is the current date and time.If year is before 1904, time stamp is negativ

inputs.name: UTC format
inputs.description: UTC format specifies if the output string is in Universal Time or in the configured time zone for the computer. If TRUE, date/time string is in Universal Time.The default is FALSE.

outputs.name: date/time string
outputs.description: date/time string is the formatted date/time string.

name: Get Waveform Components
description: Returns the analog waveform you specify. You specify components by clicking on the center of the output terminal and selecting the component you want.

inputs.name: waveform
inputs.description: waveform is the waveform from which you want to retrieve components.

outputs.name: t0
outputs.description: t0 returns the trigger time of the waveform.

outputs.name: dt
outputs.description: dt returns the time interval in seconds between data points in the waveform.

outputs.name: Y
outputs.description: Y returns the data values of the waveform.

outputs.name: attributes
outputs.description: attributes returns the names and values of all waveform attributes.You also can use the Get Waveform Attribute VI to retrieve the names and values of all attributes or the value of a single attribute.

name: Mantissa & Exponent Function
description: Returns the mantissa and exponent of the input numeric value such that number = mantissa * 2^exponent.

inputs.name: number
inputs.description: number can be any decimal representation.

outputs.name: mantissa
outputs.description: mantissa has the same decimal representation as number.

outputs.name: exponent
outputs.description: exponent has the same decimal representation as number.

name: Equal? Function
description: Returns TRUE if x is equal to y. Otherwise, this function returns FALSE. You can change the comparison mode of this function.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x is a value to compare to y

inputs.name: y
inputs.description: y must be of the same type as x.

outputs.name: x = y?
outputs.description: When you compare arrays, x = y? is a scalar in Compare Aggregates mode and a Boolean array in Compare Elements mode (default).

name: Select Function
description: Returns the value wired to the t input or f input, depending on the value of s. If s is TRUE, this function returns the value wired to t. If s is FALSE, this function returns the value wired to f.

inputs.name: t
inputs.description: t is the value that this function returns if s passes a TRUE value.t and f must be of the same type, but they can have different numeric representations.

inputs.name: s
inputs.description: s determines whether the function returns the value of t or f in s? t:f.If you wire an error cluster to s and an error occurs, the error cluster passes a TRUE value to the function. Otherwise, the error cluster passes a FALSE value to the function.

inputs.name: f
inputs.description: f is the value that this function returns if s passes a FALSE value.t and f must be of the same type, but they can have different numeric representations.

outputs.name: s? t:f
outputs.description: s? t:f is the value wired to t if s is TRUE. s? t:f is the value wired to f if s is FALSE.

name: Global Variable
description: Use global variables to access and pass data among several VIs.

name: Rotate Right With Carry Function
description: Rotates each bit in value one bit to the right (from most significant to least significant), inserts carry in the high-order bit, and returns the least significant bit.

inputs.name: carry
inputs.description: carry is the new high-order bit of value.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: value must be an integer. It cannot be an array or a cluster.

outputs.name: lsb carry out
outputs.description: lsb carry out is the former low-order bit of value.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: value is the new value.The data type of the output value is determined by the data type of the input value.

name: Boolean Array To Number Function
description: Converts a Boolean array to an integer or a fixed-point number by interpreting the array as the binary representation of the number. The first element of the array corresponds to the least significant bit in the number.

inputs.name: Boolean array
inputs.description:
Boolean array is a one-dimensional array of Boolean values.
    This function truncates Boolean array if it is too long. If Boolean array is too short, this function pads Boolean array according to the Sign Extension Mode you can select by right-clicking the function.
      
        Never —This func

outputs.name: number
outputs.description:
number is an integer or a fixed-point number.
    You can change the numeric representation of number on the Output Configuration page of the Properties dialog box.  Right-click the function and select Properties to display the Properties dialog box.

name: Hyperbolic Cosecant Function
description: Computes the hyperbolic cosecant of x.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: csch(x)
outputs.description: csch(x) is of the same numeric representation as x.

name: Inverse Hyperbolic Cotangent Function
description: Computes the inverse hyperbolic cotangent of x.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: argcoth(x)
outputs.description: argcoth(x) is of the same numeric representation as x.

name: VISA Close Function
description: Closes a device session or event object specified by VISA resource name.

inputs.name: VISA resource name
inputs.description: VISA resource name specifies the resource to be opened. The VISA resource name control also specifies the session and class.

inputs.name: error in (no error)
inputs.description:
error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. With the following exception, this input provides standard error in functionality.
    This node runs normally even if an error occurred before this node runs.

outputs.name: error out
outputs.description: error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality.

name: Expression Node
description: Use the Expression Node to calculate expressions that contain a single variable. The following built-in functions are allowed in formulas: abs, acos, acosh, asin, asinh, atan, atanh, ceil, cos, cosh, cot, csc, exp, expm1, floor, getexp, getman, int, intrz, ln, lnp1, log, log2, max, min, mod, rand, rem, sec, sign, sin, sinc, sinh, sizeOfDim, sqrt, tan, tanh.

inputs.description: input is the value that the Expression Node uses as the variable.

outputs.description: output returns the value of the calculation.

name: Get Matrix Diagonal
description: Returns the diagonal of matrix beginning at the index (row, column).

inputs.name: matrix
inputs.description: matrix must be a 2D array of any numeric type or a real or complex matrix.

inputs.name: index (row)
inputs.description: row must be an integer or real number. row is the starting row index in the output. The default is 0.

inputs.name: index (col)
inputs.description: column must be an integer or real number. column is the starting column index in the output. The default is 0.

outputs.name: diagonal
outputs.description: matrix diagonal is the matrix or 2D array that contains the diagonal elements in matrix.

name: Logarithm Base X Function
description: Computes the base x logarithm of y.

inputs.name: y
inputs.description: y can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: logx(y)
outputs.description:
logx(y) is the logarithm of y to the base x. When x and y are complex, the following equation defines logx(y):
    logx(y) = ln(y)/ln(x)
    Refer to the Natural Logarithm function for more information.

name: Hyperbolic Tangent Function
description: Computes the hyperbolic tangent of x.

inputs.name: x
inputs.description: x can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: tanh(x)
outputs.description: tanh(x) is of the same numeric representation as x.

name: To Extended Precision Float Function
description: Converts a number to an extended-precision, floating-point number.

inputs.name: number
inputs.description: number can be a scalar number, array or cluster of numbers, array of clusters of numbers, and so on.

outputs.name: extended precision float
outputs.description: extended precision float is of the same data type structure as number.

name: Close File Function
description: Closes an open file specified by refnum and returns the path to the file associated with the refnum.

inputs.name: refnum
inputs.description: refnum is the file refnum associated with the file you want to close.

inputs.name: error in
inputs.description:
error in describes error conditions that occur before this node runs. With the following exception, this input provides standard error in functionality.
    This node runs normally even if an error occurred before this node runs.

outputs.name: path
outputs.description: path is the corresponding path to refnum.

outputs.name: error out
outputs.description: error out contains error information. This output provides standard error out functionality.
